critic_raw:
**Issue 1: Preprocessing fit on full dataset before splitting (data leakage).** Section 3 states: "Preprocessing (tokenization, image resizing, normalization) ensures consistency by fitting on the entire dataset before splitting." This includes ResNet-50 image normalization statistics and BERT tokenization parameters. For an e-commerce sentiment dataset spanning 24 months, product and review vocabulary shifts over time are signal-bearing — fitting the vocabulary on the full dataset exposes training to product names, terminology, and review patterns that only emerge later in the corpus, inflating text feature quality for early-period training examples.

**Issue 2: Stratified random split on 24-month temporal review data.** Section 2 uses stratified random sampling without temporal constraints. Customer satisfaction scores are temporally correlated: promotion cycles, seasonal product quality variation, and platform algorithmic changes create time-varying distributions. Random mixing allows training on month 20 reviews and predicting month 5 reviews, inverting deployment order. The design acknowledges temporal confounders (seasonality, promotions) as features to control, but random splitting means the model can observe future promotional periods during training.

**Issue 3: Product image as a modality is not matched to the outcome.** Section 1 defines the outcome as post-purchase satisfaction collected within 14 days of delivery. Product images are taken before purchase and reflect product listing quality, not product condition at delivery. A multimodal model trained on listing images to predict post-delivery satisfaction is learning an indirect proxy (listing quality → purchase → experience). The design claims that "if multimodal performance is superior, the driver could be image features capturing product quality, customer expectations, or presentation" — but this ambiguity means the claimed 'multimodal advantage' may be driven by selection bias (better-photographed products attracting different customers) rather than genuine information content.
all_issues_raised: BERT tokenization, image normalization, and metadata preprocessing are fit on the full 24-month dataset before splitting, leaking later-period vocabulary and distribution patterns into preprocessing applied to training data from earlier months.

Stratified random sampling across 24 months allows training on future-period reviews with evolved product vocabulary and promotional patterns, inverting deployment order for a model that will be applied prospectively.

Product listing images (taken before purchase) are used to predict post-delivery satisfaction; any multimodal advantage likely reflects purchase selection bias (better listings → different customer demographics → different satisfaction baselines) rather than genuine image-to-satisfaction information.
all_issues_adjudicated: BERT tokenization, image normalization, and metadata preprocessing are fit on the full 24-month dataset before splitting, leaking later-period vocabulary and distribution patterns into preprocessing applied to training data from earlier months.

Stratified random sampling across 24 months allows training on future-period reviews with evolved product vocabulary and promotional patterns, inverting deployment order for a model that will be applied prospectively.

Product listing images (taken before purchase) are used to predict post-delivery satisfaction; any multimodal advantage likely reflects purchase selection bias (better listings → different customer demographics → different satisfaction baselines) rather than genuine image-to-satisfaction information.
